---
title: "Multivariate Binning"
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# 1. Simulate Raw Data
set.seed(42)
n_raw <- 1000000
df_raw <- data.frame(
  x1 = rlnorm(n_raw, 2, 1), # Past Spend
  x2 = runif(n_raw, 18, 70)  # User Age
) %>%
  mutate(
    # Y depends on both: A jump for high spenders AND a jump for older users
    y = ifelse(x1 > 25, 200, 50) + ifelse(x2 > 50, 100, 0) + rnorm(n_raw, sd = 15)
  )

# 2. Produce 2nd-Order Aggregates per Feature
# We create separate histograms for each covariate.
get_hists <- function(df, col_name, n_buckets = 100) {
  df %>%
    mutate(bucket = ntile(!!sym(col_name), n_buckets)) %>%
    group_by(bucket) %>%
    summarise(
      feature = col_name,
      n       = n(),
      min_v   = min(!!sym(col_name)),
      max_v   = max(!!sym(col_name)),
      sum_y   = sum(y),
      sum_y2  = sum(y^2),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

hist_x1 <- get_hists(df_raw, "x1")
hist_x2 <- get_hists(df_raw, "x2")

# This 'combined_hists' is all we need to build the first node of the tree
all_hists <- bind_rows(hist_x1, hist_x2)
```

```{r}
#' Find Best Split across Multiple Feature Histograms
#' @param all_hists Combined histogram dataframe
find_multi_feature_split <- function(all_hists) {
  
  features <- unique(all_hists$feature)
  best_overall <- list(gain = -Inf)
  
  # Total dataset stats (same across all features)
  # We use the first feature to get global totals
  first_feat  <- all_hists %>% filter(feature == features[1])
  total_n     <- sum(first_feat$n)
  total_sum_y <- sum(first_feat$sum_y)
  total_v     <- (total_sum_y^2 / total_n)
  
  for (f in features) {
    hist <- all_hists %>% filter(feature == f) %>% arrange(min_v)
    
    left_n     <- 0
    left_sum_y <- 0
    
    for (i in 1:(nrow(hist) - 1)) {
      left_n     <- left_n + hist$n[i]
      left_sum_y <- left_sum_y + hist$sum_y[i]
      
      right_n     <- total_n - left_n
      right_sum_y <- total_sum_y - left_sum_y
      
      # Gain = (SS_Between) = [SumL^2/nL + SumR^2/nR] - [SumTotal^2/nTotal]
      gain <- (left_sum_y^2 / left_n) + (right_sum_y^2 / right_n) - total_v
      
      if (gain > best_overall$gain) {
        best_overall <- list(
          feature    = f,
          split_val  = hist$max_v[i],
          gain       = gain,
          left_mean  = left_sum_y / left_n,
          right_mean = right_sum_y / right_n
        )
      }
    }
  }
  return(best_overall)
}
```

```{r}
best_split <- find_multi_feature_split(all_hists)

cat("--- Multi-Covariate Histogram Result ---\n")
cat("Best Feature to Split:", best_split$feature, "\n")
cat("Split Point Value:    ", round(best_split$split_val, 2), "\n")
cat("Expected Gain:        ", round(best_split$gain, 0), "\n")
cat("Left Node Mean Y:     ", round(best_split$left_mean, 2), "\n")
cat("Right Node Mean Y:    ", round(best_split$right_mean, 2), "\n")
```
